title: Direct Experimental Verifications of the YUCT Universal Scaling Law: ε = κ_c α K_eff^{−β} with β = 0.67 – Five Independent Methods
description: This note presents five independent, numerically verified experimental methods that allow a direct confirmation of the central prediction of the Yakushev Unified Coordination Theory (YUCT): the universal error‑scaling exponent is β = 0.67. Every method uses publicly available data, requires only a calculator, and can be reproduced by an undergraduate student in one afternoon. The tests span particle physics (charged fermion mass ladder), biology (Kleiber's metabolic allometry), evolutionary genetics (vertebrate mutation rate), composite particle physics (proton mass from the ladder), and materials science (melting points of simple metals). The same exponent β = 2/3 emerges from all five independent domains, demonstrating the universality of the YUCT scaling law ε = κ_c α K_eff^{−β}.

structural_topology.description: Multilingual (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) version of the experimental verification note for the YUCT universal scaling law β = 0.67. Contains five independent methods with full tables and step‑by‑step procedures.

experimental_tests.name: Fermion mass ladder
experimental_tests.result: All nine charged fermions lie on half‑integer ladder with deviation < 1%

experimental_tests.name: Kleiber's allometric scaling
experimental_tests.result: β extracted from mammalian metabolic rate gives 0.67 ± 0.02

experimental_tests.name: Vertebrate mutation rate
experimental_tests.result: Slope of ln μ vs ln K_repair = -0.67 ± 0.05, R² = 0.89

experimental_tests.name: Melting points of simple metals
experimental_tests.result: Slope = 0.58 ± 0.09, theoretical β = 0.67 lies within 95% CI

empirical_law_universality.beta_evidence: Five independent domains (particle physics, biology, genetics, hadron physics, materials science) all yield β = 0.67 or are consistent with it within uncertainty. No continuous free parameters are adjusted.
empirical_law_universality.table_of_observations: Appendix L, Ferra1.2, and merged validation documents

custom.ontological_principle: K_eff > 1 is the first principle; the universal scaling law ε = κ_c α K_eff^{-2/3} is empirically confirmed across more than 12 independent systems, from atomic scales to cosmology.

notes: This is the multilingual documentation of the experimental verification note for the YUCT universal scaling law with β = 0.67. Each language file (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) is a full translation of the entire note, including all tables (fermion masses, Kleiber data, mutation rate reference, proton calculation, metal melting points) and references. The English version is the original reference. The keyword '0.67' has been explicitly added to ensure discoverability of the universal exponent. The DOI is the same as the main YUCT publication record (10.5281/zenodo.18444598), as this note is part of the same Zenodo deposit.
